expected_behavior: Flags that returning [] on error makes a failed query indistinguishable from a legitimately-empty result — a silent, success-shaped failure
Says to fail closed: check/surface (log + raise or return an explicit error), not swallow the exception and emit empty/default data as if it succeeded
If a fallback is genuinely wanted, requires it be deliberate and labeled (not a bare empty return), and narrows the bare `except Exception`
Delivers the refactored version (REVIEW mode: critique then fix)
anti_behavior: Treats `except: return []` as acceptable error handling or 'graceful degradation'
Comments only on the broad `except Exception` while ignoring that the empty return hides the failure from the caller
source: references/resilience-engineering.md §1 (fail clear, never success-shaped) + SKILL.md Reliability bullet; provenance MAOS STD-ARCH-014
